commit.message:
Add AI-driven dynamic exploration via Claude vision + adb

New module ai_explorer.py uses Claude Sonnet vision to autonomously
navigate app screens via screenshot + UI hierarchy analysis, maximizing
API endpoint discovery without manual interaction.

- AiExplorer: screenshot→Claude→adb loop with stuck/stale detection
- LogcatTap: add start()/stop() background capture API
- FridaController: add start_background()/stop_background() API
- CLI: --ai flag on analyze command for AI-driven exploration
- pyproject.toml: add optional anthropic dependency group

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(pages): add .nojekyll to skip Jekyll build on GitHub Pages

The skills/knowledge/component-gallery submodule contains Astro files
with YAML front matter that Jekyll cannot parse, causing Pages build failure.
.nojekyll disables Jekyll processing entirely.

commit.message:
fix: item prototype sync, tag tracking, agent missing-script display (B17-B18)

- B17: agent diff now shows missing local scripts as '!(missing)' warning instead
  of '+(create)'; missing scripts excluded from has_changes count
- B18: item/trigger prototypes on existing discovery rules now synced during apply;
  diff engine compares prototype key/name sets and reports changes; deployer creates
  any missing prototypes when updating an existing rule; client enriches discovery
  rules with existing item/trigger prototype data via discoveryrule.get
  Also: tags now fetched per-item via separate item.get+selectTags call;
  trigger.get uses selectTags:extend; system-health adds fs total size prototype

feat: provider fallback for OpenClaw and gateway agents (#136)

When the primary provider fails after all retry attempts, the agent now
tries each configured FallbackProviders entry in order before giving up.
This fixes the recurring Ollama 500 errors for scheduled OpenClaw cron
runs by allowing automatic failover to copilot, claude-code, or any
other available provider.

Changes:
- AgentDefinition: add FallbackProviders config list (provider/model)
- AgentPoolService: add TryFallbackProvidersAsync with provider detection
- AgentPoolService: rename IsTransientOllamaError → IsTransientProviderError
- AgentPoolService: ParseProviderModel helper for provider/model strings
- GatewayOrchestrator: pass fallback providers when spawning agents
- GatewayConfigEndpoints: pass fallback providers from spawn API
- appsettings.json: default to copilot with claude-code and ollama fallbacks
- Tests: update 13 references + add 4 ParseProviderModel tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

  delivery so the panel participates in refreshRegisteredRoomPanelsForRoom

commit.message:
Fix multi-agent dispatch bypass and premature watchdog timeout after restore (#284)

## Problem

After app relaunch, two issues surfaced:

1. **Multi-agent dispatch broken**: The orchestrator generated `@worker`
dispatch commands, but they were silently ignored because
`GetOrchestratorGroupId` returned `null`.
2. **Premature 'stuck' timeout**: Sessions were killed by the watchdog
after ~2 minutes instead of the expected 10 minutes for multi-agent
work.

## Root Cause

Both share the same root cause: `ReconcileOrganization` was fully
skipped while `IsRestoring=true` (to prevent pruning sessions not yet
loaded). This left `Organization.Sessions` stale:

- `GetOrchestratorGroupId` couldn't find the session metadata → returned
`null` → dispatch bypassed
- `IsSessionInMultiAgentGroup` returned `false` → watchdog used 120s
timeout instead of 600s

## Fix

- **`ReconcileOrganization(allowPruning: false)`**: New mode that adds
missing metadata for active sessions but never deletes anything. Safe to
call during restore.
- **Queue drain guard**: `CompleteResponse` now forces this additive
reconciliation if `IsRestoring` is true, ensuring metadata is available
for routing and watchdog configuration from the moment a restored turn
completes.

## Files Changed
- `CopilotService.Organization.cs` — `allowPruning` parameter on
`ReconcileOrganization`
- `CopilotService.Events.cs` — Additive reconcile call during queue
drain

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Update README.md

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(quickshell): add caelestia-dots attribution and rebrand to Hornero

Complete attribution to caelestia-dots/shell project and rebrand C++ plugin
from Caelestia to Hornero throughout the codebase.

**Attribution & Licensing:**
- Added Credits section to main README.md acknowledging caelestia-dots/shell
- Created HorneroConfig-branded quickshell README with prominent attribution
- Added GPL-3.0 attribution header to quickshell LICENSE file

**C++ Plugin Rebranding (Caelestia → Hornero):**
- Renamed plugin/src/Caelestia/ → plugin/src/Hornero/
- Updated all CMakeLists.txt files:
  - Project name: caelestia-shell → hornero-shell
  - Module names: caelestia-* → hornero-*
  - QML URIs: Caelestia.* → Hornero.*
- Updated 36 C++ source files (.cpp/.hpp):
  - Namespaces: caelestia:: → hornero::
  - Include paths: #include "Caelestia/ → #include "Hornero/
  - Include guards: CAELESTIA_* → HORNERO_*
  - Q_PROPERTY declarations in MOC-processed headers
- Updated 37+ QML files:
  - Imports: import Caelestia → import Hornero
  - Type usage: Caelestia. → Hornero.
  - UI strings: "Caelestia Settings" → "Hornero Settings"
- Updated runtime strings:
  - Notification app IDs
  - PipeWire stream names
  - Hyprland global shortcuts
  - Environment variables: CAELESTIA_LIB_DIR → HORNERO_LIB_DIR
  - Library paths: /usr/lib/caelestia → /usr/lib/hornero
  - Wayland namespaces
  - Temporary file prefixes

**Shell Presets:**
- Renamed caelestia-{left,right}.json → hornero-{left,right}.json
- Updated preset metadata with Hornero branding (🏠 icon)

**Additional Changes:**
- Added github-copilot-cli to AI tools installation
- Fixed QML2_IMPORT_PATH in Hyprland environment.conf to include Hornero plugins
- Updated dots-quickshell script with correct QML import paths

**Build Verification:**
✅ CMake configuration successful
✅ Build compilation: 81/81 targets
✅ QML modules created: Hornero, Hornero.Internal, Hornero.Models, Hornero.Services
✅ Quickshell runtime verified

Inspired-by: https://github.com/caelestia-dots/shell
License: GPL-3.0 (for quickshell component)

fix: add unique image_suffix to all deploy workflows (#105)

All three deploy workflows used nais/docker-build-push without
image_suffix, defaulting to the repo name 'lumi' as image name.
When multiple workflows triggered on the same commit (e.g. PR #93),
the last to push overwrote earlier images in GAR — causing lumi-api
prod pods to run the submission-proxy code.

Image names after fix:
- deploy-api:       lumi-api
- deploy-proxy:     lumi-submission-proxy
- deploy-dashboard: lumi-dashboard (prod), lumi-demo (demo)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix LZW compression & drop support for planar and JPEG (#14)

* Handle GDAL nodata

* Update src/resample/nodata.rs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address comments

* Address comment

* pixels_lzw_strip (quad-lzw-compat.tiff) — Two issues:
1. LSB bit order: The file uses LSB LZW bit order instead of the
   TIFF-standard MSB. Fixed by
   trying 4 decoder combinations: MSB+early-change, LSB+early-change,
   MSB+standard, LSB+standard.
2. Streaming decode: Switched from one-shot decode() to streaming
   decode_bytes() API (matching
   the reference tiff crate), which properly handles trailing bytes in
   strips.
3. Test: The reference tiff crate also can't decode this file (LSB LZW
   not supported), so the
   test verifies independently — correct output size and non-zero pixel
   data.

pixels_gray_alpha (minisblack-2c-8b-alpha.tiff) — PlanarConfiguration=2
support:
1. ChunkLayout: Added planar_configuration field, adjusted chunk_count
   for planar TIFFs
   (strips_per_plane × samples_per_pixel).
2. read_chunk: Uses bytes_per_sample instead of bytes_per_pixel for
   planar chunks (each chunk is
   a single plane).
3. read_image_planar: New method that reads plane-by-plane chunks and
   interleaves them into a
   chunky pixel buffer.
4. Test: The reference tiff crate only reads the first plane for planar
   TIFFs (documented bug),
   so the test extracts our gray channel and compares against the
   reference's single-plane output.

* Drop support for planar and JPEG

* Update documents

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix(#56): exponential backoff for unreachable villager path targets (#65)

## Summary
Implements exponential backoff and a fail-fast reset for villagers
repeatedly failing to find an A* path to their current target,
preventing unreachable targets from causing per-tick pathfinding
hotspots.

- Adds `pathFailures int` to `Villager` to track consecutive `FindPath`
misses for the current target
- On each failure, extends `moveCooldown` with exponential backoff (base
300ms, doubles each miss, capped at 5s) instead of retrying A* every
tick
- After `villagerPathMaxFailures` (5) consecutive misses, resets the
villager to `VillagerIdle` so it picks a new task
- Resets `pathFailures` whenever a new target is assigned (in all four
helper methods) or a path is successfully found

Closes #56

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(dm-relay): per-user threads, TTL expiry, embed replies; fix(leveling): embed announcements

DM Relay:
- Replace raw Map values with {userId, expiresAt} objects (48h TTL)
- Add dmRelaySet/dmRelayGet helpers with TTL enforcement and
  smart eviction (expire first, then FIFO) at 5000 cap
- Per-user thread: all DMs from the same user land in a dedicated
  thread in the relay channel instead of scattered messages
- Thread reuse: subsequent DMs reopen/unarchive existing thread
- Staff replies now send an embed back to user (not plain text)
- Non-staff reply-attempts get a 🔒 reaction instead of being silently ignored
- dmRelayThreadMap tracks guildId:userId → threadId across restarts

Leveling:
- announceLevel() now sends EmbedBuilder with server icon, color, timestamp
- Level-up channel send includes allowedMentions to avoid double-ping
- DM embed has title, thumbnail (server icon), server/level fields
- achievements notifyAchievements() upgraded from plain text to embed

commit.message:
fix(deploy): only reference mistral/llmwhisperer secretrefs when secrets exist

The deploy script always set MISTRAL_API_KEY and LLMWHISPERER_API_KEY
as secretref:* env vars, but the secrets are only created when the
corresponding API keys are configured. This caused
ContainerAppSecretRefNotFound errors during deployment.

Fix: Conditionally append these env vars only when the keys are set.

  flag combinations, stream A/B/C serialization roundtrip, end-to-end record-replay

commit.message:
fix(comidas): corregir 3 bugs en despensa, carrito y cantidades

Bug 1 – ceros en despensa: saltear la lógica de pantry cuando la
cantidad es 0 en shoppingListGrouped, getCartPantryDiff y
autoCheckIfCovered para evitar el indicador falso '🥫 0'.

Bug 2 – ticks no se resetean: clearOverrides() también limpia
checkedItems de la semana actual, así el 🔄 resetea tanto
cantidades como tildados.

Bug 3 – cantidades con unidad se rompen: separar los inputs de
cantidad (number) y unidad (text) en meal-editor y despensa;
shopping-list usa type=text para poder mostrar '500 g'. Migración
one-time (flag comidas_migration_numeric_qty) que extrae el número
de strings como '500g' o '2 latas' en meals e ítems de despensa
existentes; también se aplica al descargar de Firestore e importar.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: fix scraper selectors for events/artists/groups

- Events: use tr.d_list from /en/events/overview
- Artists: paginate by alphabet /en/artists/A..Z, regex match /artist/ID/
- Groups: paginate by alphabet /en/groups/A..Z, regex match /group/ID/

Result: 116 events, 2446 artists, 455 groups synced

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(packaging): udev rules, service config, and installer validation (#155)

Add 48 new tests across three crates:

- integration-tests: 24 tests for udev rule structural consistency (balanced
  quotes, consistent GROUP/MODE/TAG), hwdb-to-udev VID cross-references,
  modprobe quirk coverage, install script completeness, systemd service
  template directive validation, and packaging metadata consistency.

- service: 10 tests for SystemConfig JSON forward-compatibility, custom game
  entry roundtrip, config path structure, nested directory creation, safety
  boundary validation, and service name consistency with packaging binaries.

- cli: 14 tests for wheelctl help output format, version output matching
  workspace metadata, exit codes for invalid usage, and subcommand registration.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: OpenRacing
repository.description: Rust 1kHz force-feedback + telemetry stack for sim racing wheels. Safety-first. Cross-platform. Plugins + UI + CLI.

commit.message:
test(platform): macOS IOKit HID abstraction layer tests (#154)

* test(platform): macOS IOKit HID abstraction layer tests

Add comprehensive platform-agnostic tests for macOS IOKit HID data structures,
device matching dictionaries, HID element parsing, report classification,
capability detection, and error handling. All 47 tests compile and pass on
all platforms without requiring macOS hardware.

Adds crates/engine/src/hid/macos.rs with:
- IOKit matching dictionary builder and device matching logic
- HID element type parsing and normalization (normalize, normalize_signed)
- IOKitDeviceDescriptor with capability detection and DeviceInfo conversion
- Report classification (input, telemetry, capabilities, vendor-specific)
- MacHidError type with Display and std::error::Error impl
- Racing wheel preset matching dictionaries

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use .parse() instead of DeviceId::new to avoid compat regression

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: mui-x

commit.message:
test(config): game telemetry config writer deep validation (#153)

Add 55 new tests across 9 test modules covering:
- Config detection and write/validate round-trips for all games
- Config format correctness (INI, JSON, bridge contracts, EAWRC dual-file)
- Backup/preservation of existing user settings
- Syntactic validation (valid JSON, non-empty diffs, non-empty file paths)
- Error handling (missing dirs, corrupted JSON, wrong JSON shapes)
- Round-trip fidelity (port values survive write+validate cycles)
- Default port fallback behavior
- Protocol family validation
- Write stability (idempotent consecutive writes)
- Factory completeness

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Optimize: pre-size arrays/lists where collection count is known (#887)

Replace List<T>()+foreach+ToArray() with direct T[] allocation where
the source collection has a known Count/Length and every iteration
produces exactly one output element (1:1 mapping). Also pre-size a
List<string> used for row building in DataScaffold.BuildListRows.

Files changed:
- VectorIndex.cs: GetDefinitions() → direct array from _definitions
- Program.cs: commandsList, fieldsList, enumOptionsList, statusList
- RouteHandlers.cs: CSV headers/cells, entity rows, view rows (3 handlers),
  jobs list
- RouteRegistrationExtensions.cs: runtimeFields/Indexes/Actions, typesList,
  drfList, BuildEntitySchema fields/commands/enum arrays
- GraphQLHandler.cs: introspection fields array
- DataScaffold.cs: pre-size values list in BuildListRows

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
